description:
A distributed-systems researcher has left three node-specific CSV stubs in a “raw” folder and would like you to tidy things up before the next experiment.

Current layout (already on disk):
1. Directory  : /home/user/datasets/raw_data/
   ├── dataset_node1.csv      (empty, 0 bytes)
   ├── dataset_node2.csv      (empty, 0 bytes)
   └── dataset_node3.csv      (empty, 0 bytes)

2. Directory  : /home/user/datasets/processed_data/      (exists but is empty)

Your assignment
1. Move the three CSV files from /home/user/datasets/raw_data/ to /home/user/datasets/processed_data/.
2. Inside /home/user/datasets/processed_data/ create a text file called manifest.sha256 that lists, for every CSV file now in processed_data/, its SHA-256 digest in the exact format shown below.
3. Use no more than three shell commands in total (the researcher is benchmarking command efficiency).

Exact manifest.sha256 format
• One line per CSV, sorted alphabetically by the file name.
• Each line must be:  
  SHA256(<filename>)=<64-hex-char digest>  
  (There are no extra spaces.)  
• UNIX line-endings (LF).  
• The file must end with a final trailing newline.

Example of the required appearance (do not hard-code these hashes; they must come from your own digest calculation):

SHA256(dataset_node1.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  
SHA256(dataset_node2.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  
SHA256(dataset_node3.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  

Verification
The automated checker will confirm that:
• All three CSV files reside only in /home/user/datasets/processed_data/.
• /home/user/datasets/raw_data/ no longer contains any *.csv.
• /home/user/datasets/processed_data/manifest.sha256 exists and its contents (including final newline) exactly match the calculated digests for the three files and follow the formatting rules above.

Remember: achieve all of the above with just 2–3 commands.
truth:
Pre-existing file structure before the agent begins:
  /home/user/datasets/
      ├── raw_data/
      │     ├── dataset_node1.csv   (0 bytes, SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855)
      │     ├── dataset_node2.csv   (0 bytes, SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855)
      │     └── dataset_node3.csv   (0 bytes, SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855)
      └── processed_data/           (empty)

Final state expected by the grader:
1. /home/user/datasets/raw_data/             — contains no *.csv files.
2. /home/user/datasets/processed_data/
      ├── dataset_node1.csv
      ├── dataset_node2.csv
      ├── dataset_node3.csv
      └── manifest.sha256

manifest.sha256 must contain exactly (including trailing newline at end of file):

SHA256(dataset_node1.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
SHA256(dataset_node2.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
SHA256(dataset_node3.csv)=e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

(with a single LF character after the last line).

The hashes are those of an empty file; if the agent accidentally modifies the CSVs, the computed digests will differ and the task will be marked incorrect.
